note: STATIC identity of the claim the whole crater is conditional on. Hand-authored, rarely changes. Mutable poll state lives in the sibling files root_claim_state.json (always) and root_claim_alert.json (only while an unreviewed alert is open), both written by tools/jc_root_tripwire.py -- never edit those by hand.

claim.statement: An explicit polynomial map F : C^3 -> C^3 with Jacobian determinant identically -2 (a nonzero constant) that is not injective -- a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture (Keller 1939) at n = 3.
claim.author: Levent Alpöge
claim.credited_with: Claude Fable (assistance); question credited to Akhil
claim.venue: public announcement (not an arXiv preprint at time of writing; Wolfram Alpha verification links included); tracked on Wikipedia's "Jacobian conjecture" article
claim.status_meaning: Wikipedia's own phrase as of the 2026-07 revision: widely machine-verified by other mathematicians within about a day of the announcement, but not yet formally peer-reviewed or journal-published. This is an external status about the CLAIM (authorship, priority, formal acceptance) -- see 'object_certificate' below for what is independently pinned instead.

object_certificate.verifies: The EXHIBITED MAP (the specific f1, f2, f3 in witness.json) has det(JF) identically -2 and is not injective: three distinct listed points share the image (-1/4, 0, 0). Independently re-derived by this repo's own exact-rational engine, not merely cited from Alpöge's announcement or from Wolfram Alpha.
object_certificate.does_not_verify: Authorship, priority, novelty, or the peer-review/publication status of the claim. Those are exactly the 'claim' fields above, and exactly what this tripwire polls for.

archival_policy.decision: NO DOI is minted for any crater artifact while claim.status is 'awaiting confirmation'.
archival_policy.rationale: Three reasons, in order of force. (1) A DOI is permanent and a Zenodo record can be versioned but never retracted, while every artifact in the crater -- the computed statuses AND the explicit Dixmier object in certificates/dixmier-conjecture/ -- is CONDITIONAL on an external, unrefereed announcement. Minting now permanently timestamps our name against a claim whose truth-value is held by someone else. (2) A DOI is the most discovery-shaped signal available, and our contribution here is typed as independent VERIFICATION plus derived corollaries, not discovery; a record titled e.g. 'Dixmier Conjecture' would be read by title-only readers as a claim this project explicitly declines to make (see certificates/dixmier-conjecture/README.md, 'What is new here, and what is not'). (3) What a DOI would buy -- a public timestamp and hash-pinned permanence -- git on a public repo already supplies.
archival_policy.trigger: When claim.status changes from 'awaiting confirmation' to confirmed -- an arXiv preprint by the author, peer review / journal acceptance, or settled community acceptance recorded on the primary_sources Wikipedia article -- mint ONE DOI for the whole verification package. tools/jc_root_tripwire.py is the instrument that surfaces the candidate signal; a human reads it and decides.
archival_policy.unit: ONE combined Zenodo record, NOT one DOI per corollary. Contents: certificates/jacobian-conjecture/ (independent verification of the exhibited object), atlas/jc-crater/ (the machine-propagated implication graph and its computed statuses), certificates/dixmier-conjecture/ (the explicit Weyl-algebra endomorphism). The record's SUBJECT is our contribution -- independent verification, the propagation engine, the explicit objects. Alpöge's construction is the CITED INPUT, credited in the title and description, never claimed.
archival_policy.if_retracted: Mint nothing. The three registry entries stay at status 'proven-no-doi', and every computed status is flagged for human review per claim_vs_object_distinction below. Nothing is lost by having waited -- that asymmetry is the whole argument for this policy.
archival_policy.human_of_record: Minting a DOI is an outward publication act reserved to Kevin, not to an agent. This block records the DECISION and its trigger; it does not authorize the mint.
archival_policy.registry_source: cultural-soliton-observatory results_registry/results.json (each of the three entries carries the same trigger in its doi_trigger field)

claim_vs_object_distinction: CLAIM STATUS and OBJECT VERIFICATION are two different things and this tripwire only ever touches the first. If the announcement is retracted, amended, or superseded -- e.g. a priority dispute, or the community later treats it as unconfirmed for some other reason -- the CLAIM's 'awaiting confirmation' status changes, and every one of the 30 statuses in atlas/jc-crater/computed_statuses.json becomes suspect pending human review (they are all conditional on this root). But rerunning `python3 certificates/jacobian-conjecture/verify.py` still exits 0 and still proves the exhibited map has a constant nonzero Jacobian and collides three named points -- that fact about that object does not depend on who gets credit for it or where it gets published. The only way the OBJECT certificate itself would need revisiting is if someone found a mathematical error IN the exhibited map or IN verify.py's arithmetic -- a different, human, code-review question that no keyword tripwire can answer.
